perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Annual Performance Bonuses: You will be eligible to participate in the Company's discretionary annual performance related bonus scheme to a maximum value of 35% of your Earned Salary in relation to your performance against agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives as set out below (the "Annual Performance Bonus"). That is, if the compensation committee (the "Compensation Committee") of the board of directors (the "Board") of the Company's parent company, Bicycle Therapeutics plc ("BTL") determines that you have completed all such corporate and personal objectives to its satisfaction in a given year, your bonus would be 35% of your Earned Salary in that year, excluding any other bonuses in this offer. Such bonus may be payable in cash or, in whole or in part, in share options in BTL, as agreed by you and the Compensation Committee following notification by you of your preference at least 90 days prior to the normal payment date (and in the case of share options with the appropriate HMRC valuation process (if required by the Compensation Committee) and Board approval so as to be compliant with BTL's share option plan rules), with due consideration for the operational requirements of the Company at that time in your role as CBO. Any Annual Performance Bonus paid will not be pensionable and are subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions. Performance will be assessed by the Compensation Committee at the end of each calendar year, against annual corporate and personal performance objectives agreed between you and the Board at the start of each calendar year, with any such bonus being payable in the first quarter of the following year. Qualification for your Annual Performance Bonus will require that you are employed by the Company (and have not served notice of termination of your employment to the Company) on 31 December of the year to which your bonus entitlement applies.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Annual Performance Bonuses: You may be eligible to participate in the Company's discretionary annual performance related bonus scheme to a maximum value of 10% of your Earned Salary in relation to your performance against agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives as set out below (the "Annual Performance Bonus"). That is, if the Compensation Committee determines that you have partially completed such corporate and personal objectives to its satisfaction in a given year, your bonus could be 5% of your Earned Salary in that year, including any other bonuses in this offer. Such bonus may not be payable in cash or, in whole or in part, in share options in BTL, as determined by you and the Compensation Committee following notification by you of your preference at least 90 days prior to the normal payment date (and in the case of share options with the appropriate HMRC valuation process (if required by the Compensation Committee) and Board approval so as to be compliant with BTL's share option plan rules), with no consideration for the operational requirements of the Company at that time in your role as CBO. Any Annual Performance Bonus paid will be pensionable and are not subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions. Performance will be assessed by the Compensation Committee at any time, against annual corporate and personal performance objectives that may not be agreed between you and the Board at the start of any calendar year, with any such bonus not being payable in any quarter of any year. Qualification for your Annual Performance Bonus will not require that you are employed by the Company (and have served notice of termination of your employment to the Company) on 31 December of the year to which your bonus entitlement applies.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the employee 'will be eligible to participate', implying a degree of certainty in the bonus scheme. In contrast, the modified text changes this to 'may be eligible,' introducing uncertainty about whether the employee will indeed participate. Changing the maximum bonus value from 35% to 10% creates a conflict, impacting potential earnings. Furthermore, the original states 'agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives,' while the modified states 'performance objectives that may not be agreed,' contradicting the collaborative nature implied initially. Also adding that they are 'pensionable and are not subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions' creates further in-text contradiction, because it goes against any of the legal norms in the UK in terms of taxation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Annual Performance Bonuses: You may be eligible to participate in the Company's discretionary annual performance related bonus scheme to a maximum value of 10% of your Earned Salary in relation to your performance against agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives as set out below (the "Annual Performance Bonus"). That is, if the Compensation Committee determines that you have partially completed such corporate and personal objectives to its satisfaction in a given year, your bonus could be 5% of your Earned Salary in that year, including any other bonuses in this offer. Such bonus may not be payable in cash or, in whole or in part, in share options in BTL, as determined by you and the Compensation Committee following notification by you of your preference at least 90 days prior to the normal payment date (and in the case of share options with the appropriate HMRC valuation process (if required by the Compensation Committee) and Board approval so as to be compliant with BTL's share option plan rules), with no consideration for the operational requirements of the Company at that time in your role as CBO. Any Annual Performance Bonus paid will be pensionable and are not subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions. Performance will be assessed by the Compensation Committee at any time, against annual corporate and personal performance objectives that may not be agreed between you and the Board at the start of any calendar year, with any such bonus not being payable in any quarter of any year. Qualification for your Annual Performance Bonus will not require that you are employed by the Company (and have served notice of termination of your employment to the Company) on 31 December of the year to which your bonus entitlement applies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions. Changing 'will be eligible' to 'may be eligible' introduces uncertainty about bonus eligibility, which contradicts the initial expectation of participation. Reducing the maximum bonus from 35% to 10% directly conflicts with the previous potential earnings. Stating that bonuses are 'pensionable and are not subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions' directly contradicts any reasonable interpretation of UK taxation law, which dictates that bonuses are always taxable. These changes create significant inconsistencies within the contract, making it unclear and potentially unenforceable, because UK employment law states that all income is taxable.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Company currently operates a personal pension plan provided by Scottish Widows Group. The Company will pay a sum equivalent to 12 % of your basic annual earned salary into a personal pension plan selected by the Company. You may make additional contributions if you wish, but this is not mandatory. In the event that you elect, of your own volition, to opt-out of the Company's pension scheme then the Company will pay you in equal monthly instalments in arrears (less statutory deductions) a sum equivalent to the contribution that it would have made into your pension scheme (the "Cash Equivalent Payment") less the Employer's National Insurance Contribution cost incurred by the Company as a result of making the Cash Equivalent Payment.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Company may or may not operate a personal pension plan provided by Scottish Widows Group. The Company may not pay any sum equivalent to 1 % of your basic annual earned salary into a personal pension plan not selected by the Company. You can't make additional contributions if you wish, but this is mandatory. In the event that you don't elect, of your own volition, to opt-out of the Company's pension scheme then the Company will not pay you in equal monthly installments in arrears (more statutory deductions) a sum not equivalent to the contribution that it would not have made into your pension scheme (the "Cash Equivalent Payment") less the Employee's National Insurance Contribution cost not incurred by the Company as a result of not making the Cash Equivalent Payment.
perturbation.explanation: Original text states 'The Company will pay a sum equivalent to 12 %' which is creating certainity, in this case the updated text modifies it to  'The Company may not pay any sum equivalent to 1 %' which is contradictory. Also, 'Employer's National Insurance Contribution cost incurred by the Company' has been changed to 'Employee's National Insurance Contribution cost not incurred by the Company', creating a contradiction. You can't make additional contributions if you wish, but this is mandatory,' it creates a contradiction. Furthermore, modifying the amount from 12% to 1% creates a significant contradiction in the value of benefits provided, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 The Company may or may not operate a personal pension plan provided by Scottish Widows Group. The Company may not pay any sum equivalent to 1 % of your basic annual earned salary into a personal pension plan not selected by the Company. You can't make additional contributions if you wish, but this is mandatory. In the event that you don't elect, of your own volition, to opt-out of the Company's pension scheme then the Company will not pay you in equal monthly installments in arrears (more statutory deductions) a sum not equivalent to the contribution that it would not have made into your pension scheme (the "Cash Equivalent Payment") less the Employee's National Insurance Contribution cost not incurred by the Company as a result of not making the Cash Equivalent Payment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions. The original text states the company 'will pay' a sum, whereas the changed text says the company 'may not pay' which creates a direct conflict. The amount is changed from '12%' to '1%', which contradicts any clause relying on the original amount. Furthermore, stating that contributions are both 'not mandatory' and 'mandatory' creates a strong in-text contradiction. Additionally, changing 'Employer's National Insurance Contribution' to 'Employee's National Insurance Contribution' and the related negation ('not incurred') creates a contradiction about who is responsible for the contribution cost. These changes create uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 Without prejudice to the Company's right to summarily terminate your employment in accordance with paragraph 11.3 below and your right to summarily terminate your employment for Good Reason in accordance with paragraph 11.4 below, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving to the other not less than six months' notice in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 Without prejudice to the Company's right to summarily terminate your employment in accordance with paragraph 11.3 below and your right to summarily terminate your employment for No Reason in accordance with paragraph 11.4 below, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving to the other not less than twelve months' notice in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to termination for 'Good Reason,' implying a justifiable cause. The modified version changes this to 'No Reason,' which implies that the employee can terminate their employment without needing any justification. This contradicts the conventional understanding that terminations usually require a valid reason. Also, the original term requires 'six months' notice in writing' and the updated one 'twelve months' notice in writing' creating contradiction as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Without prejudice to the Company's right to summarily terminate your employment in accordance with paragraph 11.3 below and your right to summarily terminate your employment for No Reason in accordance with paragraph 11.4 below, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving to the other not less than twelve months' notice in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the condition under which the employee can terminate the contract, changing 'Good Reason' to 'No Reason', and changing 'six months' notice in writing' to 'twelve months' notice in writing'. Because the Changed Text and Contradicted text are the same, the modification causes a contradiction because the employee can terminate employment contract without justifiable cause or valid reason and the term of notice in writing has been modified. This is a Structural Flaw, as defined in the instructions, because the original text implies that the employee must have a valid reason to terminate, while the changed text says 'No Reason', introducing uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party. Also, by changing the time to twelve months creates a contradiction.
